title: Fix invoice title, add custom labor on job, add tax field, and remove Google Review link

requirements.text: On the Invoice/Estimate preview page (InvoicePreviewPage.tsx), change the displayed business name / invoice title to exactly 'Reliable Home Appliance Repair LLC'. This string must appear as the primary heading on the printed/preview invoice and in the downloaded PDF export.

quotes: you have to change the invoice title to "Reliable Home Appliance Repair LLC"

acceptanceCriteria: The invoice preview page displays 'Reliable Home Appliance Repair LLC' as the primary business name heading.
The downloaded PDF includes 'Reliable Home Appliance Repair LLC' as the invoice title.
No other business name string appears as the primary heading.

requirements.text: On the Job Detail page (JobDetailPage.tsx), ensure the Labor section supports fully manual/custom labor line item entry without requiring a predefined labor rate. The inline 'Add Labor' form must include: a free-text name field, a type selector (hourly or flat), an hours field (shown only when hourly is selected), an editable rate/amount field (dollars, not required to match any saved rate), and an optional description field. Each added line item must display its computed amount (hours × rate for hourly, flat amount for flat) and a remove button. Show a running labor subtotal below the list. Persist line items via the existing addLaborLineItem and removeLaborLineItem backend endpoints.

quotes: still missing the labor rates that can be added manually or custom
Easy work flow as possible- I want it like this -add client-add job-parts from inventory - labor on job- add tax

acceptanceCriteria: The 'Add Labor' form on Job Detail accepts a free-text name with no requirement to select a predefined rate.
Type selector supports 'hourly' and 'flat' options.
Hours field is visible only when 'hourly' is selected.
Rate/amount field is fully editable per line item.
Computed amount is displayed per line item.
A running labor subtotal is shown below all line items.
Line items are saved via addLaborLineItem and removed via removeLaborLineItem.
No predefined labor rate selection is required to add a line item.

requirements.text: Add a configurable tax rate field to the job detail and invoice/estimate workflow. On the Job Detail page, display a tax rate input (percentage, default 8.875%) that is applied to the combined total of parts subtotal and labor subtotal to compute the tax amount and final post-tax total. Display the pre-tax subtotal, tax amount (labeled with the rate, e.g. 'Tax 8.875%'), and the post-tax total on both the Job Detail page and the Invoice/Estimate preview page. The tax rate value should be editable by the user on the job/invoice screen (not a global setting) and should be included in the PDF export.

quotes: Easy work flow as possible- I want it like this -add client-add job-parts from inventory - labor on job- add tax

acceptanceCriteria: A tax rate input field (percentage) is present on the Job Detail page, defaulting to 8.875%.
The user can change the tax rate value for the job.
Pre-tax subtotal (parts + labor) is displayed.
Tax amount is computed as (parts subtotal + labor subtotal) × (tax rate / 100) and displayed with the rate label.
Post-tax total is displayed.
The Invoice/Estimate preview page renders the tax rate, tax amount, and post-tax total.
The downloaded PDF includes all three values (pre-tax subtotal, tax, post-tax total).

requirements.text: Remove all Google Review URL references, buttons, default URL values, and related logic from the entire frontend codebase (ClientDetailPage.tsx, JobDetailPage.tsx, and any other file where a Google Review URL default, button, or link appears). If the Google Review feature is causing build instability, remove it completely rather than patching it. After removal, no 'Request Google Review' button, link, or URL constant should remain anywhere in the application.

quotes: If the Google review link is messing up the code remove it.

acceptanceCriteria: No 'Request Google Review' button or link exists on the Client Detail page.
No 'Request Google Review' button or link exists on the Job Detail page.
No Google Review URL default constant or variable exists anywhere in the frontend source.
The build completes without errors related to Google Review URL.
The Client Detail form no longer includes a googleReviewUrl input field visible to users (the field may remain in the data model if needed for backward compatibility, but the UI must not surface it).

requirements.text: Verify and ensure the job photo blob storage section on the Job Detail page (JobDetailPage.tsx) is fully operational. The 'Photos' section must display thumbnail previews of all stored job photos, include a 'Take Photo / Upload' button using an HTML file input with accept='image/*' and capture='environment', read the selected file as an ArrayBuffer, convert it to a Blob, and call addJobPhoto. Each thumbnail must include a delete icon that calls removeJobPhoto. If this section is not currently rendering or is broken, re-implement it using the existing addJobPhoto and removeJobPhoto backend endpoints.

quotes: still need blob storage for pictures on job screen

acceptanceCriteria: The 'Photos' section is visibly rendered on the Job Detail page.
A 'Take Photo / Upload' button opens the device camera or file picker.
Selected images are uploaded via addJobPhoto and displayed as thumbnails.
Each thumbnail has a delete icon that calls removeJobPhoto and removes the thumbnail.
Photo thumbnails persist across page refreshes (loaded from the backend via getJob).

requirements.text: Confirm the Stripe payment section on the Job Detail page (JobDetailPage.tsx) remains visible and functional after the current changes. The section must show a 'Charge via Stripe' button that calls createPaymentIntent with the job's post-tax total (parts + labor + tax), stores the returned payment intent ID via updateJobPayment, and displays a 'Paid' / 'Unpaid' badge based on whether stripePaymentId is present. Do not remove or regress this section during the other changes in this build.
acceptanceCriteria: The Stripe payment section is visible on the Job Detail page.
'Charge via Stripe' button is present and calls createPaymentIntent.
The amount passed to createPaymentIntent reflects the post-tax total (parts + labor + tax).
The returned payment intent ID is stored via updateJobPayment.
A 'Paid' badge is shown when stripePaymentId is present; 'Unpaid' when absent.

constraints: Do not modify files listed in frontend.immutablePaths.
All Motoko logic must remain in the single backend/main.mo actor.
The stable owner principal 'q5rzs-s67ph-qtb5w-e66j5-2iqax-vlwa5-5pqxy-yosti-xhcis-ocfw6-yqe' must not be changed or overwritten.
Google Review URL must be removed entirely rather than patched if it risks build stability.
Do not regress the Stripe payment integration, signature capture, or photo blob storage already implemented.
nonGoals: Adding any new Google Review URL or replacing it with another URL.
Adding a new global labor rate settings flow — custom rates are entered per line item on the job.
Changing the backend data model for jobs beyond what is needed for tax rate display.
Adding GPS or live location tracking.
Any new authentication or role management changes.
